questions.q: What does the term 'Intellectual Property Rights' refer to?
questions.answer: The exclusive rights granted to creators of original works for their use, distribution, and sale.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a type of Intellectual Property Right?
Why do companies protect their software through patents, copyrights, and trade secrets?
questions.answer: Companies protect their software through patents, copyrights, and trade secrets to prevent unauthorized use, copying, or disclosure of their intellectual property.

questions.q: What are the main benefits of registering trademarks?
questions.answer: The main benefits of registering trademarks include obtaining exclusive rights, preventing others from using a similar mark, and increasing brand recognition.

questions.q: What is the main difference between a copyright and a patent?
questions.answer: The main difference between a copyright and a patent is that a copyright protects original literary, dramatic, musical, and artistic works, while a patent protects inventions and industrial designs.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a trade secret?
questions.answer: The formula for a soft drink.

questions.q: Why is it essential to have a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) in place?
questions.answer: It is essential to have a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) in place to protect confidential information from being disclosed to unauthorized parties.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the International Convention for the Protection of Industrial Property?
questions.answer: The purpose of the International Convention for the Protection of Industrial Property is to provide a framework for the protection of industrial property rights, including patents, trademarks, and industrial designs, across national borders.

questions.q: Which organization is responsible for administering the Madrid System for the International Registration of Marks?
What are the consequences of violating Intellectual Property Rights?
questions.answer: The consequences of violating Intellectual Property Rights can include financial penalties, legal action, damage to reputation, and loss of business opportunities.
